Internal Legal & Compliance Memo — Al-Nour Industrial Holdings (CR 2028990001) regarding potential acquisition of Al-Rimal Packaging Co. (CR 1017783452) and related disclosure under KMA Continuing Obligations.
Draft terms (non-binding MOU): consideration SAR 420–460m funded via a mix of internal cash and a SAR 250m Murabaha; projected EPS uplift of SAR 0.12–0.15 in FY 1448H—this is non-public and constitutes inside information under the Kingdom Markets Authority (KMA) Market Conduct Regulations.
Blackout window: Insiders are in a closed period from 15 days before quarter end until publication of results on the Najd Exchange (the Exchange); no dealing is permitted until a formal announcement is released.
Selective disclosure is prohibited; any lender or adviser access is under NDA and a need-to-know roster; if any information is inadvertently shared externally, an immediate corrective announcement via the Exchange’s disclosures portal is required.
Public-status note: Our 10 Sha‘bān 1447H notice on the Exchange regarding renewal of a Shari‘a-compliant working capital facility remains public and is not MNPI for this transaction.
Press references to sector energy-price adjustments in last year’s ministerial briefings are stale and not determinative; do not treat general media coverage as a basis for disclosure delay.
Personal data handling: Mr. Faisal Al‑Mutlaq (Iqama 77-554433-219), CFO, and Ms. Hessa Al‑Nafea (Iqama 66-112233-408), Strategy Lead, are designated deal-team controllers under PDPL; limit use of their identifiers and do not transmit ID scans over email.
Cross-border transfers: Any HR or KYC files shared with our UAE payroll processor, DunePay FZ‑LLC, require a transfer impact assessment and prior permit from the National Data & AI Directorate (NDAD) per PDPL; store copies on KSA-hosted systems only.
Data minimization: remove Iqama/CR numbers from draft board packs where not legally required; use role-based IDs in the diligence dataroom and mask dates of birth.
Subtle materiality: the draft resin-supply renegotiation with Desert Polymers (7% input cost reduction from 1 Dhū al‑Qa‘dah) is operational but likely material for Al‑Nour’s margins; treat as MNPI until contract execution and announcement.
Issuer-relative materiality: although the target’s revenues are modest at SAR 95m, the transaction triggers a material development for Al‑Nour under the Continuing Obligations due to segment concentration.
Disclosure timing: if the board approves on Monday, plan an Exchange announcement before market open the next trading day; if closing occurs during trading hours, coordinate with the Exchange for an immediate posting to avoid disorderly trading.
Insider list: update the named-insider register and send a blackout reminder today; any research interactions must be scripted and confined to public information only.
Contact: Dr. Noura Al‑Saif, Chief Compliance Officer, compliance@alnour-industrial.invalid; please route any external inquiries through Legal before responding.
